IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect and maintain the electrical equipment in your area.
As part of this programme, we need to replace and upgrade a transformer mounted on an electricity pole. This important piece of equipment reduces the voltage of electricity so it can be safely supplied to homes and businesses.  To carry out this work safely, we will need to temporarily switch off your electricity, as it is not safe to work on electrical equipment while it is live. We will remove the existing transformer and install a new one on the same day. We understand that power interruptions are inconvenient, and we will do everything we can to keep disruption to a minimum. This work is taking place in the rear of Willow Farmhouse and the farmer field near The Foresters (B4011).

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by Simon at 21:47
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Our estimated time frame of 02:00-03:00 hasn't changed, but if this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. MainMessage: We are aware of an incident caused by Emergency Isolation affecting the electricity supply in your area. Our engineers are dealing with this, but we are sorry we can't give you a time for the restoration of supply at present.  Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 05:23. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
